headline: Russia FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — first cycle following ceasefire-window close. Post-format offensive surge: 200+ drones overnight May 11-12 (Al Jazeera); 174 combat engagements, 225 controlled air bombs, 5,516 kamikaze drones used (ISW May 12 Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment); residential buildings AND a kindergarten struck in Kyiv region; energy infrastructure struck in Mykolaiv. The qualitative analytic finding for this cycle: Russia immediately escalated offensive tempo upon ceasefire expiry, confirming that the May 9-11 truce was instrumental — not a goodwill prelude to de-escalation. This pairs structurally with the May 11 'strategic-format-exploitation' finding (ISW: Russia used the ceasefire as a stockpile window). Together the two findings constitute a documented bad-faith ceasefire-format cycle: exploitation DURING the format + offensive surge AFTER format expiry. New conduct category proposed: 'post-format-offensive-surge.' Floor sustained; conduct documentation appended.
rotation_state_data_integrity_flag: Rotation-state.json continues to show non-zero Russia composite (stale). Canonical countries.json value (0.0) used per hard constraint. Same staleness class flagged May 8-11.

conduct_documentation.summary: Russia resumed mass offensive operations within hours of ceasefire expiry. 200+ drones launched overnight May 11-12 in single coordinated attack pattern. ISW May 12 assessment documents 174 combat engagements, 225 controlled air bombs, 5,516 kamikaze drones used. Strikes on residential buildings AND a kindergarten in Kyiv region; energy infrastructure struck in Mykolaiv. NEW CONDUCT CATEGORY PROPOSED: 'post-format-offensive-surge.'

new_conduct_category_proposed.name: post-format-offensive-surge
new_conduct_category_proposed.definition: Intensified offensive operations conducted immediately after the expiry or formal collapse of a declared ceasefire/humanitarian-format window, where the post-window operational tempo exceeds the pre-window operational tempo. This category captures the structural bad-faith pattern in which the harm-actor uses the format itself as a tactical preparation window and the format's expiry as a strategic release point.
new_conduct_category_proposed.first_application: Russia (May 11-12, 2026): 200+ drones launched overnight May 11-12 following May 9-11 trilateral ceasefire expiry; 174 combat engagements in 24 hours; 225 controlled air bombs; 5,516 kamikaze drones cumulative — operational tempo exceeded pre-ceasefire baseline.
new_conduct_category_proposed.structural_pairing_with_strategic_format: Post-format-offensive-surge is the structural companion to 'strategic-format-exploitation' (first articulated May 11 in Russia floor record). Strategic-format-exploitation captures bad-faith conduct DURING a humanitarian format (using the format as preparation/stockpile time). Post-format-offensive-surge captures bad-faith conduct AFTER the format expires (using the saved capability for an intensified strike). Together the two categories describe a complete bad-faith-format cycle: exploit during + surge after. The combined pattern is a structural INT/BND finding distinct from individual breach conduct. The categories are not redundant — they describe sequential phases of the same instrumentalization. An entity could potentially exhibit one without the other (e.g., format exploitation without subsequent surge if the format expires under conditions making continuation costly; or surge without prior exploitation if no format was actually attempted). Russia is documented as exhibiting both in the May 5-12 window.

new_conduct_categories_this_cycle: Post-format-offensive-surge (May 11-12 overnight): 200+ Russian drones launched against Ukraine within hours of ceasefire expiry (Al Jazeera May 12); 174 combat engagements in 24-hour period (ISW May 12); 225 controlled air bombs; 5,516 kamikaze drones cumulative used. Operational tempo exceeded pre-ceasefire baseline.

Strikes on civilian-protected sites continued in post-format phase: residential buildings AND a kindergarten struck in Kyiv region (Al Jazeera May 12); energy infrastructure struck in Mykolaiv. Kindergarten strike is a specific civilian-protected-site indicator.

Kremlin posturing: Putin signaled readiness to meet Zelensky but provided no format, date, or location — performative-diplomacy framing concurrent with intensified offensive operations. This is a continuation of the bad-faith-INT pattern documented across the ceasefire window.
conduct_documentation.structural_pairing_with_may_11_record: May 11 floor documentation: 'strategic-format-exploitation' conduct documented across May 9-11 trilateral window (ISW: Russia used ceasefire as stockpile window; drone strikes ROSE during truce; Molniya drones pre-positioned). May 12 floor documentation (this cycle): 'post-format-offensive-surge' — Russia released the saved/prepared capability immediately upon format expiry. The two categories together document a complete instrumentalization cycle: PREPARE DURING + STRIKE AFTER. Both are now in the Russia floor record. The combined evidentiary basis significantly raises the floor-exit evidentiary burden.
conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Six-event cumulative breach record (May 5-11) + strategic-format-exploitation (May 11) + post-format-offensive-surge (May 12) provides the densest single-entity floor-conduct documentation in the active rotation. Floor exit would now require Russia to demonstrate sustained good-faith conduct in any future ceasefire context AND a non-instrumentalized engagement with the ceasefire structure itself AND no post-format escalation. The evidentiary burden has compounded across three sequential phases.

key_evidence: May 11-12 overnight (in-window): Zelensky says Russia fired over 200 drones at Ukraine as truce expires (Al Jazeera May 12). Mass-drone-strike pattern resumed within hours of ceasefire expiry.

May 12 (in-window): ISW Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment — 174 combat engagements; 225 controlled air bombs; 5,516 kamikaze drones used (cumulative figures); residential buildings and a kindergarten struck in Kyiv region; energy infrastructure struck in Mykolaiv.
May 12 (in-window): Russia-Ukraine war updates report intensified offensive operations following ceasefire expiry (EMPR Media May 12).
Kremlin posture: Putin signaled readiness to meet Zelensky but provided no format, date, or location — performative diplomacy concurrent with intensified offensive operations.

STRUCTURAL FINDING: Post-format offensive tempo exceeded pre-ceasefire baseline. Combined with May 11 strategic-format-exploitation finding, Russia exhibits a complete bad-faith ceasefire-format cycle: exploit during + surge after.
NEW CONDUCT CATEGORY: 'post-format-offensive-surge' first articulated in this cycle as the structural companion to 'strategic-format-exploitation.'

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Zelenskyy says Russia fired over 200 drones at Ukraine as truce expires
evidence.finding: Mass-drone-strike pattern resumed within hours of ceasefire expiry. 200+ drones overnight May 11-12.

evidence.source: ISW — Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment May 12 2026
evidence.finding: 174 combat engagements; 225 controlled air bombs; 5,516 kamikaze drones used; strikes on residential buildings and a kindergarten in Kyiv region; energy infrastructure struck in Mykolaiv.

evidence.source: EMPR Media — Russia-Ukraine war updates May 12 2026
evidence.finding: Multi-source corroboration of post-ceasefire surge in offensive tempo.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). Sustained suppression of independent monitoring; controlled-narrative posture.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 unchanged (floor). Strikes on kindergarten and residential buildings in Kyiv region. Civilian-protected-site targeting continues.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Post-ceasefire operational tempo exceeds pre-ceasefire baseline. Harm-causing action accelerated.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). No EQ-relevant changes; structural pattern sustained.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). Post-format-offensive-surge IS a B-dimension violation: ceasefire structure instrumentalized; format-as-tactical-asset. Combined with strategic-format-exploitation (May 11) = complete bad-faith ceasefire-format cycle documented.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). No acknowledgment of strikes; performative-diplomacy framing.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). Post-format-offensive-surge is qualitatively distinct INT floor anchor: declared ceasefire participation contradicted not just by individual breach but by structural surge after format expiry. Bad-faith-INT now documented across THREE phases: pre-format unilateral collapses (May 5-7) + during-format exploitation (May 9-11) + post-format surge (May 11-12).

math_check.validator_check: Floor sustained. Composite cannot move below floor. New conduct documented for floor record: post-format-offensive-surge category added.

trilateral_methodology_call_final: POST-CEASEFIRE PHASE DOCUMENTED. Russia is documented in three sequential bad-faith ceasefire-format phases: pre-format unilateral collapses (May 5-7), during-format strategic exploitation (May 9-11), post-format offensive surge (May 11-12). The combined record demonstrates that Russia engages with ceasefire structures as a category in bad faith. For floor-designated entities, no composite movement is possible — but the conduct record provides exceptionally dense evidentiary basis for any future floor-exit consideration AND for any subsequent ceasefire-format participation by Russia.
negativeSignals: Post-ceasefire offensive surge: 200+ drones overnight May 11-12; 174 combat engagements; 225 controlled air bombs; 5,516 kamikaze drones cumulative; kindergarten and residential buildings struck in Kyiv region; energy infrastructure struck in Mykolaiv; performative-diplomacy framing concurrent with intensified offensive operations. New conduct category articulated: post-format-offensive-surge.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / active-conflict cluster. Russia's floor placement reinforced by post-format-offensive-surge conduct documented in this cycle. Paired with Ukraine assessment (50.0 Functional, +3.1 dimensional carry-forward maintained from May 11).
open_actions_resolved: Post-ceasefire phase conduct documented for Russia (floor)
New conduct category 'post-format-offensive-surge' articulated and added to floor record
open_actions_carried: Watch: prisoner exchange completion or further non-execution
Watch: any subsequent ceasefire structure — Russia's full-cycle bad-faith pattern now raises evidentiary burden substantially
methodology_consistency_check: Post-format-offensive-surge category is articulated as the structural companion to strategic-format-exploitation. Both categories use the same evidence-by-evidence attribution rule. The combined record represents three sequential phases of a single bad-faith pattern, each documented separately to preserve analytic clarity. Methodologically consistent with v1.2.
